key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can learn to map composite microstructure images directly to stress and strain fields, acting as a fast surrogate for expensive FEM simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid field prediction for physics-informed composite design, supporting scalable evaluation of microstructure and boundary-condition variants.
method: Input: geometry images of two-phase composite microstructures
Model: UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer-based global context
Output: predicted stress/strain field maps across 10 field types
evidence_signal: The model achieved strong results on most subdatasets, including R2=0.9991 and SSIM=0.9936 on periodic tessellations, with MAE below 0.05 on six of eight evaluated subdatasets.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse inclusions, indicating difficulty capturing sharp stress discontinuities in some geometries.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
